ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Orthodontic extraction.
Examination: Teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45 show proper crown eruption with visible ink-stained discoloration on occlusal surfaces, rough texture upon probing, negative percussion, and no discomfort on thermal tests.
Radiographic Examination: CBCT shows complete root development for teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Diagnosis: Teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45 identified for orthodontic extraction.
Treatment Plan: Extraction of teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Treatment Recommendations: Extraction of teeth 14, 24, 35, and 45.
Management: For teeth 24 and 35, after povidone-iodine disinfection, 1.7ml of 4% Articaine with epinephrine was administered for infiltration anesthesia, gingival separation was performed, teeth were luxated and extracted, and hemostasis was achieved with cotton ball pressure.
Medical Instructions: Follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151) with pigmentation. Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling and air polishing.
Examination: Generalized gingival swelling with easy bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets detected. Presence of calculus and pigmentation.
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151) with pigmentation.
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling and air polishing.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis and consented to treatment. Plaque disclosing, full-mouth ultrasonic scaling, air polishing, fluoride varnish application, and iodine glycerin were performed. Oral hygiene instruction (OHI) was provided, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with mouthwash. Regular check-ups and follow-up if discomfort occurs.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013). Treatment: Orthodontic principles include aligning and leveling the dental arches, leveling the Spee curve, correcting intermaxillary relationships, expanding the dental arch, retracting molars, advancing the maxillary arch, inhibiting mandibular growth, increasing anterior and posterior tooth height, and interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: clear aligners.
Examination: Overbite: normal, Overjet: normal. Skeletal Class III, mandibular plane angle is average. Anterior open bite: normal. Dental crowding: maxillary arch normal, mandibular arch normal. Occlusal plane: normal. Maxillary arch form: oval, Mandibular arch form: square. Palate: high vault. Lingual frenulum attachment: acceptable. Mandibular retrusion: possible. Oral hygiene: average, Oral mucosa: normal. Tongue: normal. Speech: normal. Maxillary midline: normal, Mandibular midline: normal. Maxilla: normal, Mandible: normal. Alveolar bone: maxilla underdeveloped, mandible underdeveloped. Facial symmetry: symmetrical. Profile: concave. Facial height: short. Lip-tooth relationship: normal. Upper lip: full. Lower lip: full.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: Orthodontic principles include aligning and leveling the dental arches, leveling the Spee curve, correcting intermaxillary relationships, expanding the dental arch, retracting molars, advancing the maxillary arch, inhibiting mandibular growth, increasing anterior and posterior tooth height, and interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: clear aligners.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Performed photography, oral scanning, radiographs, and treatment planning.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs, schedule next appointment.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013). Treatment: Orthodontic treatment plan includes alignment and leveling of dental arches, leveling of the Spee curve, correction of anterior deep bite, distalization of molars, and interproximal enamel reduction.
Examination: Normal overbite and overjet; skeletal Class III with low mandibular plane angle; normal anterior open bite; normal arch form; high palatal vault; acceptable lingual frenulum attachment; retrusive mandible; general oral hygiene; normal oral mucosa, tongue, and speech; symmetrical face with normal lip-tooth relationship.
Radiographic Examination: Presence of wisdom teeth in positions 18, 28, and 48.
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: Orthodontic treatment plan includes alignment and leveling of dental arches, leveling of the Spee curve, correction of anterior deep bite, distalization of molars, and interproximal enamel reduction.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Photographs, radiographs, and intraoral scans were taken to develop a treatment plan.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs. Schedule a follow-up appointment.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Examination: The retraction is effective.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Teeth 11, 12, 21, 22, 31, 32, 41, and 42 were banded together from canine to canine on both arches. The mandibular arch had power chains replaced to enhance retraction.
Medical Instructions: 1. Avoid biting hard or sticky foods. 2. Schedule follow-ups every 3-4 weeks. 3. Return for a check-up if there is any discomfort, tooth mobility, or appliance detachment.
Remarks:

Treatment: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct intermaxillary relations, expand the dental arch, distalize molars, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, increase anterior and posterior tooth height, perform interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: Invisible aligners.
Examination: Normal overbite and overjet. Skeletal Class III with a normal mandibular plane angle. Normal anterior open bite. Normal dental arch crowding. Normal occlusal plane. Maxillary arch is ovoid, mandibular arch is square. High palatal vault. Acceptable lingual frenulum attachment. Retrusive mandible. General oral hygiene is average, with normal oral mucosa, tongue, and speech. Normal upper and lower midlines. Normal maxilla and mandible. Alveolar bone is underdeveloped in both arches. Symmetrical face with concave profile and short facial height. Normal lip-tooth relationship with full lips.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).
Treatment Plan: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct intermaxillary relations, expand the dental arch, distalize molars, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, increase anterior and posterior tooth height, perform interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: Invisible aligners.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Photographs, oral scans, and radiographs were taken to formulate a treatment plan.
Medical Instructions: Return for follow-up if discomfort occurs and schedule regular check-ups.
Remarks:
